page: Microsoft Azure F4as_v6 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the Fasv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at a base frequency of 3.7 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache configuration consists of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU tests placing in the top 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth presents a notable tradeoff: while small-block cached read bandwidth and large-block write bandwidth are average, large-block uncached read bandwidth is weak, scoring in the bottom 25%. In text processing and single-threaded bzip3 decompression, the server demonstrates strong performance, while database operations, static web serving, and software compilation remain in the average tier.

The F4as_v6 offers a balanced resource density with its 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that benefit from high single-core clock speeds without requiring massive memory footprints or local storage. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing power. This instance is suitable for general-purpose applications, web serving, text processing, and lightweight single-threaded tasks, though its weak large-block uncached memory read bandwidth may limit its efficiency in memory-intensive database workloads.
description: The Microsoft Azure F4as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Fasv6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores, 4 vCPUs, and 16.0 GB of RAM, with no local storage or GPU. The instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core results in the top 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. However, multi-core benchmarks and large-block uncached memory read bandwidth are average and weak, respectively. This configuration provides a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that prioritize single-threaded execution speed, such as text processing, lightweight web serving, and basic compilation tasks, while avoiding the overhead of unneeded local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F4as_v6 is a Fasv6-family virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, suitable for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F4as_v6 is a 4-vCPU virtual machine with an AMD EPYC processor, 16 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated four-core virtual machine featuring high single-threaded performance and a balanced memory profile for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Four dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong text processing capabilities
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage or GPUs
Average multi-core performance and weak large-block uncached memory read bandwidth
Best suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and single-threaded tasks
